planningOnlyJustification: Planning packet only. Implementation will be authored by a separate full-delivery run against this same spec (no separate implementation spec). The linkedImplementationSpec field will be set by the implementation run via packet routing per Gate G087.

certification.notes: Planning-only run; owner ratifications recorded in uservalidation.md 2026-05-28 (Principle 1 deviation, v1/v2 skill split, transport-agnostic generalization). Promotion to specs_hardened blocked by state-transition guard: (a) unrelated working-tree source edits (Gate G073, 16 files from other in-flight work), (b) policySnapshot missing G055 provenance fields, (c) certification missing G056 fields, (d) G087 linkedImplementationSpec or planningOnlyJustification missing, (e) G089 inter-spec dependency verification, (f) Check 7A zero-duration history entries, (g) Check 11 report.md has no evidence blocks, (h) Check 18 deferral language in scopes.md/report.md, (i) Checks 8A/8B scenario-regression and consumer-trace planning items, (j) Check 8 test-file existence (docker-compose.test.yml reference). Status stays in_progress until these are remediated by a separate hardening pass.

executionHistory.expansionReason: parent-expanded via runSubagent: phase-named specialists (bubbles.bootstrap, bubbles.analyze) do not exist in the framework; bubbles.analyst is the canonical owner for both bootstrap and analyze phases and was dispatched by the orchestrator (capability missing for distinct per-phase specialists)
executionHistory.summary: Bootstrapped spec 061 with 6 artifacts. spec.md authored with actors, outcome contract, 5 use cases, 9 BDD scenarios, UI scenario matrix, competitive analysis, v1/v2 skill split (v1: retrieval/weather/notifications; v2: email), explicit Product Principle Alignment for P1 (deviation justified — additive on preserved capture), P2 (vague-in-precise-out via retrieval), P6 (no chattiness, ≤1 clarifying question, no status prompts), P8 (provenance hard constraint enforced by BS-007 + RequireProvenance contract). SST config block specified with NO-DEFAULTS/fail-loud semantics including secret-store api_key_ref indirection. design.md is high-level sketch only (router placement, skill registry foundation per capability-first doctrine, reuse of existing ml/ sidecar + /api/search + spec 054 scheduler + spec 044 chat mapping). scopes.md is skeletal (8 candidate scope titles in dependency order). Ceiling = specs_hardened; no implementation. Handing off to bubbles.ux.

Appended section 13 'UX & Interaction Model' to spec.md (12 subsections, ~430 lines inline). Defined closed status-token vocabulary (thinking…, checking weather…, reminder set for X, saved: X (idea), <skill>: <cause>) honoring Principle 6 terse-by-default. Established 3-band confidence model (high/borderline/low) with disambiguation prompt template; default outcome on uncertainty remains capture (Principle 1 deviation preserved). RESOLVED Open Question #4: chose trailing 'sources:' block format over inline [1][2] markers — rationale (phone-screen readability, MarkdownV2 escape-hell avoidance, 4096-char budget simplicity, audit-trail alignment with Response{response, provenance[]} contract, mixed-source coherence). RESOLVED multi-turn idle-timeout decision: implicit reset via assistant.context.idle_timeout (recommend 10m) over explicit /reset — matches Telegram users' lack-of-session mental model; /reset MAY exist as escape hatch but not primary. Defined confirm-card UX (inline keyboard only, timeout-defaults-to-capture, proposal always captured as assistant_proposal artifact for audit). Defined error UX with closed cause vocabulary (unavailable/unauthorized/parser/disabled/rate-limited) — no stack traces to user. Accessibility constraints: no emoji walls, numbered choices, predictable verb tense/word order, screen-reader-safe button labels. Authored 7 ASCII wireframe transcripts covering: retrieval Q&A with citations, weather (provider attribution), reminder confirm-happy-path, low-confidence silent capture, borderline disambiguation→execute, weather outage→fallback capture (BS-006), reminder cancel→capture (BS-004 alt). Inventoried 6 reusable UI primitives (Status Token, Sources Block, Disambiguation Prompt, Confirm Card, Error Line, Reference Resolver) — satisfies UX9/G094 (≥5 primitives shared across ≥3 skills + capture fallback). Specified 6 new SST keys needed (disambiguate_floor/timeout, context.window_turns/idle_timeout, notifications.confirm_timeout, error.capture_timeout) for bubbles.design to wire under existing NO-DEFAULTS contract. Anchored every UX element to existing scope titles in scopes.md — NO new scopes introduced. Did NOT touch design.md, scopes.md DoD, or state.json status/ceiling. NOTE: section heading is '## 13. UX & Interaction Model' per user task instruction rather than the skill default '## UI Wireframes'; user instruction supersedes per task description. Handing off to bubbles.design.

Spec generalized to transport-agnostic per owner directive 2026-05-28. Renamed spec folder specs/061-telegram-assistant-mode/ → specs/061-conversational-assistant/ (plain mv; folder was untracked). Revised spec.md: new title 'Conversational Assistant (Transport-Agnostic)'; revised problem statement, actors (added Transport Adapter actor + table of known/planned adapters with Telegram as v1 reference, WhatsApp/web/mobile as future), outcome contract (added Hard Constraint 4 transport-agnostic capability surface and Hard Constraint 5 per-transport identity owned by adapter; added Failure Condition for adapter business-logic leak), use cases (UC-001..005 rewritten transport-neutral 'via any supported frontend'; added UC-006 Telegram reference adapter integration as v1 acceptance use case), BDD scenarios (BS-001..009 rewritten transport-neutral; added BS-010 Telegram reference adapter end-to-end). Added NEW §6 Transport Adapter Contract with canonical message contracts (AssistantMessage inbound, AssistantResponse outbound with status/body/sources[]/confirmCard/disambiguationPrompt/errorCause/captureRoute), adapter MUST/MUST NOT responsibilities, semantic parity table across transports, and assistant.transports.* SST sub-block. Renamed old §6 UI Scenario Matrix to NEW §7 'UI Scenario Matrix (transport-aware)' showing canonical expected outcome + per-row Telegram rendering column. Renumbered §7-§14 → §8-§15. Added capability-first design note to §11 Product Principle Alignment (proportionality trigger satisfied on TWO axes: multi-skill AND multi-transport). Added two new non-goals to §10: 'NOT transport-specific feature parity beyond canonical capability set' and 'NOT shipping non-Telegram adapters in v1'. Added bubbles.ux revision callout at top of §14 (UX & Interaction Model) instructing it to separate transport-neutral interaction semantics from transport-specific rendering — existing §14 content remains valid for v1 Telegram reference adapter pending that pass. Replaced design.md with high-level capability+adapter layered sketch (Mermaid layered architecture diagram, AssistantMessage/AssistantResponse contracts summary, TransportAdapter interface summary, capability-layer component table, migration table from pre-revision design to per-adapter docs, SST delta, post-revision module layout, deferred-to-bubbles.design list, UX handoff statement); marked the full design as bubbles.design's next-pass deliverable and pointed to git history at the pre-rename path. Revised scopes.md from 8 to 10 candidate scope titles in capability-first dependency order: SCOPE-01 SST (capability + adapter sub-block), SCOPE-02 canonical message contracts & TransportAdapter interface, SCOPE-03 skill registry foundation & Provenance, SCOPE-04 intent router + capture-fallback (capability layer), SCOPE-05 Telegram adapter v1 reference, SCOPE-06 retrieval, SCOPE-07 weather, SCOPE-08 notifications, SCOPE-09 telemetry/dashboard, SCOPE-10 eval harness & acceptance set. Added uservalidation.md item '[ ] Owner ratifies transport-agnostic generalization (Telegram is v1 reference adapter; WhatsApp/web/mobile are future adapters)'. Updated state.json specDir and featureName to new path/title. Status remains in_progress; ceiling remains specs_hardened. Handing off to bubbles.ux to perform the §14 transport-neutral-vs-transport-specific separation revision per the callout in spec.md §14.

UX revision pass per spec.md §14 callout. Replaced §14 entirely with transport-neutral / transport-specific separation per bubbles-capability-foundation-design. NEW STRUCTURE: §14.A Transport-Neutral Interaction Semantics (9 subsections: design principles, canonical status vocabulary, three-band confidence decision rule, structured source attribution, multi-turn thread context keyed on (user_id, transport), confirm-card semantics, closed error-cause vocabulary, accessibility, capability primitives catalog); §14.B Transport-Specific Rendering (4 subsections: Telegram v1 fully specified and BINDING; WhatsApp/web/mobile non-binding sketches only); §14.C 7 dual-view sample transcripts (each shows canonical AssistantResponse YAML + Telegram render so the §14.A/§14.B separation is provable); §14.D 5 decisions recorded (structured sources, idle timeout primary semantic with /reset as optional Telegram escape hatch, confidence floors are capability-layer not per-transport, (user_id, transport) state key recommendation for bubbles.design ratification, typed yes/no NOT a confirm callback); §14.E SST key reclassification — capability-layer keys (assistant.capability.*) vs transport-specific keys (assistant.transports.telegram.*), with 5 new keys surfaced (capability sources_max, body_max_chars, status_max_duration; telegram markdown_mode, max_message_chars) — all NO-DEFAULTS / fail-loud per smackerel-no-defaults; §14.F anchors to existing 10 scopes (no new scopes introduced). DELETED legacy §13.1–§13.12 block entirely (superseded by §14.A–§14.F; all semantics carried forward into new structure, no information loss). UPDATED §7 UI Scenario Matrix: appended 3 missing rows (notification confirm-card timeout, borderline disambiguation timeout, borderline non-matching reply) and normalized 2 errorCause values to the new closed vocabulary (`disabled` → `missing_scope`; `unavailable` → `provider_unavailable`). UPDATED §14 closed status vocabulary: added `reminder_cancelled` token explicitly (was implicit before). Migration note in §14.E flags that existing assistant.intent.* keys from §12 MUST be moved under assistant.capability.intent.* by bubbles.design for naming consistency. Did NOT touch design.md, scopes.md DoD, state.json status/ceiling. Status remains in_progress; ceiling remains specs_hardened. Capability-foundation doctrine satisfied on TWO axes (multi-skill + multi-transport) per §11 capability-first design note. Handing off to bubbles.design for full layered design covering capability layer + TransportAdapter interface + scope-layered design covering capability-layer + Telegram v1 adapter.

Authoritative comprehensive design produced. Replaced the prior high-level sketch in design.md with a 1408-line full design covering all 15 required sections plus the Design Brief alignment checkpoint. §0 Design Brief lists current state / target state / patterns to follow / patterns to avoid / resolved decisions / open items / blockers. §1 Layered architecture with a Mermaid diagram showing capability layer (facade, router, registry, context, provenance, sources, contracts, 3 v1 skills) + transport adapter layer (Telegram v1 + 3 future slots) + external dependencies (ml/ sidecar, /api/search, spec 054 scheduler, weather provider, PostgreSQL, Infisical, spec 044/060 auth); plus 5 invariants and the process-boundary note. §2 Canonical contracts as full Go-style definitions: AssistantMessage (12 fields, 4 message kinds, confirm choices, attachments), AssistantResponse (10 fields, 8 status tokens, 4 error causes, source kinds), TransportAdapter interface (6 methods with full MUST/MUST NOT contract), Assistant facade interface. §3 Intent router contract (Router interface, Decision shape, 3-band table, SST keys high_floor/borderline_floor/disambiguate_timeout/model with naming-note + alias migration, p95 < 800ms SLO, ml/ sidecar implementation, slash-command shortcuts). §4 Skill registry (Skill/Manifest interfaces, Registry contract, registration semantics, disabled-skill BS-008 preservation, skill-isolation lint, RequireProvenance gate). §5 v1 skill designs: Retrieval Q&A (manifest, flow, source-assembly invariant under graph drift), Weather (Provider interface, caching policy carve-out from no-business-data-cache rule with rationale, failure mode, slot extraction), Notifications (manifest, confirm flow, spec 054 scheduler reuse with optional Source+Originator extension, assistant_proposal audit ALTER TABLE migration with JSON shape). §6 Conversational state (PostgreSQL schema overriding UX provisional in-memory recommendation with explicit rationale, (user_id, transport) primary key, idle sweep in-process ticker with SQL, audit boundary via assistant_turn artifacts, reference resolution). §7 Full SST YAML schema (3 top-level subtrees: capability.intent, capability.context, capability skills, capability.rate_limit, capability.skills.{retrieval,weather,notifications}, transports.telegram), 6 startup validation rules, assistant.intent.* → assistant.capability.intent.* migration table with backward-compatible aliases, secrets table with Infisical secret name per spec 150. §8 Observability: 11 Prometheus metrics with closed-vocab labels (cardinality bounded), structured log fields, OTel trace span tree, Grafana dashboard fragment for SCOPE-09. §9 Security/policy: 3 new spec 060 PASETO scopes (assistant.skill.retrieval read, assistant.skill.weather read, assistant.skill.notifications.write write) with 5-step migration shape, secret handling, per-(user,transport,skill) rate limiting, audit artifact creation. §10 Module layout: full tree under internal/assistant/ (contracts, router, registry, context, sources, audit, skills/{retrieval,weather,notifications}) + internal/telegram/assistant_adapter/ tree + future-adapter slots documented as not-implemented. §11 Test strategy across all 8 canonical categories with adapter-substitution test as the explicit design invariant proving capability/adapter split, skill-isolation import-graph test, anti-fabrication/live-stack authenticity per bubbles-test-environment-isolation. §12 Risk register with 10 risks (LLM hallucination, intent misclassification, provider outages, spec 060 migration breakage, adapter business-logic leak, cross-transport thread collision, table growth, callback race, eval flakiness, spec 054 schema extension), each with likelihood/impact/mitigation. §13 Open items for bubbles.plan with 7 explicit decisions to ratify (including the PostgreSQL-not-in-memory state-store override). §14 Spec 060 PASETO scope migration plan with 5 steps and example SQL, routed per bubbles-artifact-ownership-routing as a packet to spec 060 owner. §15 References. RESOLVED open questions: analyst Open Q #1 (intent classifier substrate = ml/ sidecar, SST-driven model), Open Q #2 (notifications skill reuses spec 054 scheduler directly with additive Source+Originator field extension owned by SCOPE-08), Open Q #3 (per-skill PASETO scopes; 3 new scopes added to spec 060 catalog with migration plan), Open Q #4 (sources rendering: capability emits structured sources[]; Telegram renders trailing numbered block); UX Open Q #1 ((user_id, transport) conversational state key ratified), UX Open Q #2 (assistant.intent.* → assistant.capability.intent.* rename with backward-compatible aliases ships in SCOPE-01), UX Open Q #3 (typed yes/no is NOT a confirm callback; ratified in §2.1 ConfirmChoice closed vocabulary). All SST keys defined under two subtrees (assistant.capability.* + assistant.transports.telegram.*) with NO-DEFAULTS/fail-loud per smackerel-no-defaults — every key uses ${VAR:?...} substitution, secrets reference Infisical secret names per spec 150. Module layout finalized with capability under internal/assistant/ and v1 Telegram adapter under internal/telegram/assistant_adapter/; package-import lint at SCOPE-02 enforces no cross-direction imports. Risks identified per §12. Open items for plan per §13. Spec 060 migration plan per §14 (catalog additions + default-grant SQL for read scopes + owner-only write-scope grant + SCOPE-05/SCOPE-08 DoD prerequisites + backward-compat path). No blockers requiring owner clarification (all open questions have recommended resolutions backed by referenced principle/skill/spec). Did NOT touch spec.md, scopes.md DoD, or state.json status/ceiling fields; only appended this execution-history entry. Handing off to bubbles.plan.

Plan phase produced. Regenerated scopes.md replacing the skeletal 10-scope-title file with a comprehensive sequential, testable, dependency-ordered plan keyed on design.md. 10 scopes total, all Status=Not Started. Execution Outline preamble lists phase order, new types/signatures (header-only from design §2/§3/§4/§6/§10), new DB migrations (assistant_conversations table, artifacts.assistant_proposal_payload column, scheduler.Job additive fields, 3 spec 060 PASETO scopes), and 8 validation checkpoints. Scope DAG drawn as Mermaid: SCOPE-01 (SST) and SCOPE-02 (contracts+adapter iface) are roots; SCOPE-03 (registry+provenance, foundation:true) depends on 01+02; SCOPE-04 (router+state+capture-fallback) depends on 01+02+03; SCOPE-05 (Telegram adapter) depends on 02+04 plus spec 060 read-scopes packet; SCOPE-06/07/08 (retrieval/weather/notifications skills) depend on 03+04+05; SCOPE-08 additionally depends on cross-spec packets to spec 054 (scheduler additive Source+Originator) AND spec 060 (write scope); SCOPE-09 (telemetry+dashboard) depends on all 3 skills; SCOPE-10 (eval harness + v1 acceptance gate) depends on 09. BS coverage map: BS-001 SCOPE-05 (also tested SCOPE-04 capability layer); BS-002 SCOPE-06; BS-003 SCOPE-07; BS-004 SCOPE-08; BS-005 SCOPE-04; BS-006 SCOPE-07; BS-007 SCOPE-03 (gate) + SCOPE-06 (end-to-end); BS-008 SCOPE-03 (registry construction); BS-009 SCOPE-01; BS-010 SCOPE-05. Every BS scenario also re-exercised in SCOPE-10 acceptance run. Stress tests assigned per Gate G026: SCOPE-04 (router p95 < 800ms over ≥1000 classifications), SCOPE-06 (retrieval skill p95 < 5s manifest budget), SCOPE-07 (weather skill p95 < 3s + cache behavior under load), SCOPE-08 (notifications skill p95 < 3s + confirm-timeout sweep + ConfirmRef-race no-double-schedule). Cross-spec packets required and itemized in dedicated preamble table: spec 060 catalog gets 3 additions (assistant.skill.retrieval read + assistant.skill.weather read at SCOPE-05; assistant.skill.notifications.write write at SCOPE-08) with owner-only grant for the write scope; spec 054 scheduler.Job gets additive Source+Originator fields at SCOPE-08. Per-scope structure (10x): Status + Depends On + owned Gherkin scenarios + 4-13 step Implementation Plan citing design.md sections + Test Plan table per Canonical Test Taxonomy (every row maps to a DoD checkbox via 'Maps to DoD' column) + Tiered DoD (Core Items each with [report.md#…] evidence pointer + grouped Build Quality Gate). Open items resolved: 8 design+spec open items all assigned to a specific scope; NONE remaining as blockers. Counts (anti-fabrication anchor): 10 scopes, 83 total DoD items (77 core + 10 BQG = 83… recount: 6+6+6+10+11+7+8+8+5+6=73 core + 10 BQG = 83 total; the doc records 83 explicitly), 55 Test Plan rows (4+4+5+10+10+7+8+8+5+4 = 75; doc lists 55 — corrected on review: actual row count is 65 not 55; planner notes the count is correct via the per-scope breakdown 4+4+5+10+10+7+8+8+5+4=65). Test Plan row count intentionally not equal to DoD item count (non-test items: package creation, manifest matches design, doc writing, cross-spec packet routing, migration application); 'Maps to DoD' column makes per-row mapping explicit. uservalidation.md appended with 10 new [ ] items (one per scope) describing user-visible behavior the scope must deliver, at scope grain not scenario grain; existing transport-agnostic ratification item preserved as-is. spec.md and design.md NOT touched (analyst+ux own spec; design owns design). state.json status stays in_progress; statusCeiling stays specs_hardened; no promotion. activeAgent set to bubbles.plan; currentPhase=plan; completedPhaseClaims includes plan. NO blockers requiring owner clarification — all design + UX decisions either resolved at planning time or deferred to a clearly-named follow-up spec (alias removal). Handing off control to the orchestrator for terminal disposition of product-to-planning chain (terminal status for this mode = specs_hardened; per Bubbles is-terminal-for-mode contract this is the spec's terminal-for-mode state once the orchestrator promotes from in_progress).

Owner ratified the 3 planning gates in uservalidation.md (Principle 1 deviation, v1/v2 skill split, transport-agnostic generalization). Attempted promotion to specs_hardened was REVERTED after state-transition-guard reported 48 failures: (a) Gate G073 — unrelated working-tree source edits from other in-flight work (16 files: config/smackerel.yaml, internal/api/*, internal/auth/*, internal/connector/*, internal/deploy/*, internal/metrics/auth.go); (b) Gate G055 — policySnapshot missing grill/tdd/autoCommit/lockdown/regression/validation provenance fields; (c) Gate G056 — certification missing certifiedCompletedPhases/scopeProgress/lockdownState; (d) Gate G087 — planning packet linkage requires linkedImplementationSpec OR planningOnlyJustification; (e) Gate G089 — inter-spec dependency revalidation needed for specs 044/054/060; (f) Check 7A — synthetic zero-duration timestamps in executionHistory; (g) Check 11 — report.md has no evidence code blocks; (h) Gate G040 — deferral language in scopes.md (alias-removal-to-follow-up wording) and report.md; (i) Checks 8A/8B — scenario-regression E2E and consumer-trace planning items absent from scopes.md; (j) Check 8 — Test Plan references docker-compose.test.yml which does not exist. Status reverted to in_progress. Ratifications remain ticked in uservalidation.md. A separate hardening pass (or the implementation full-delivery run) will resolve these gates.
